steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice uses the table data to complete the statement comparing mean ratings of aware-of-placebo participants versus control-group participants. The table shows three measures: Global improvement (5.0 vs 3.9), Symptom severity reduction (92.00 vs 46.00), and Quality of life improvement (11.4 vs 5.4). In every row, the aware group's number is higher.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'ranged from 5.0 to 92.00, indicating that well-being varied widely from participant to participant': The range 5.0–92.00 describes the aware group's own values across measures, not a comparison to the control group, and the conclusion about individual variation is unsupported. ✗
  • Choice B — 'were lower for two measures, with the rating for only one measure indicating greater well-being': The aware group's ratings are higher on all three measures, not just one. ✗
  • Choice C — 'ranged from 3.9 to 46.00, with no rating indicating greater well-being in any measure': Those numbers are the control group's values, and the conclusion contradicts the data entirely. ✗
  • Choice D — 'were higher for all three measures, indicating greater overall well-being for these participants': 5.0 > 3.9, 92.00 > 46.00, and 11.4 > 5.4 — the aware group's ratings exceed the control group's on every measure. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
